ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn FOODON_03301737 represents bottled water as a category; no brand-specific Dasani code exists. Closest parent used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A00LC covers bottled water generically; no Dasani-specific code exists in FoodEx2. Closest parent used.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: SNOMED CT 11713004 represents water as a substance; no bottled-water product-specific code exists. Closest parent used.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard 500 ml bottle of Dasani Drinking Water, equivalent to approximately 500 g, representing a typical single-serve portion.

nutrient_source.title: Beverages, water, tap, drinking — USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (NDB No. 14555); cross-referenced with Dasani product label (Coca-Cola Company)
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service — FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Dasani is purified water with added minerals (magnesium sulfate, potassium chloride, salt). Macronutrient profile is identical to plain drinking water: zero energy, protein, fat, and carbohydrate. Sodium value adapted from Dasani product label (approximately 10 mg per 240 ml serving, equating to ~4.2 mg per 100 g). USDA FoodData Central entry for drinking water used as primary nutrient anchor.

gi_source.title: Glycemic index of water and non-caloric beverages — University of Sydney GI Database convention; Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC. International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008. Diabetes Care. 2008;31(12):2281-2283.
gi_source.source_match_notes: Pure water contains no glycemic carbohydrates and therefore has no measurable glycemic index. By convention, glycemic_index is set to 1 (the lowest valid positive sentinel value) to satisfy schema requirements. gi_category is low. glycemic_load_per_serving is 0.0 because carbohydrate_g per serving is 0. Reference: Atkinson et al. 2008 International GI tables; University of Sydney GI Database convention for non-caloric beverages.

hypertension.key_factor: Minimal sodium content (~4.2 mg per 100 g; ~21 mg per 500 ml bottle)
hypertension.guidance: Dasani Drinking Water contains only trace sodium from added minerals and is safe for individuals with hypertension; adequate daily water intake supports blood pressure regulation and renal function.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Zero carbohydrates and zero glycemic load
type2_diabetes.guidance: Dasani Drinking Water contains no carbohydrates or sugars and has a glycemic load of zero, making it the optimal beverage choice for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes; replacing sugar-sweetened beverages with water is a primary dietary recommendation in diabetes management guidelines.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Zero fat, zero cholesterol, zero sugar; trace electrolytes
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Dasani Drinking Water contains no fat, cholesterol, or sugar, and its trace mineral content poses no cardiovascular risk; adequate hydration supports cardiac output and circulatory function in individuals with cardiovascular disease.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Dasani Drinking Water is a commercially bottled purified water product manufactured by the Coca-Cola Company and sold globally, including in Singapore via food delivery platforms such as field survey. The water undergoes reverse osmosis purification to remove impurities, after which a proprietary blend of minerals — including magnesium sulfate, potassium chloride, and a small amount of sodium chloride (salt) — is added to enhance taste. The result is a clean, neutral-tasting, calorie-free beverage with no protein, fat, carbohydrate, or sugar content. Per 100 g, Dasani provides approximately 4.2 mg of sodium and 1.6 mg of potassium from its added mineral blend, with all other macronutrients at zero. Clinically, Dasani is the ideal beverage for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes (zero glycemic load), hypertension (minimal sodium), and cardiovascular disease (zero fat and cholesterol). It is suitable for all dietary patterns including vegetarian, vegan, halal, kosher, and gluten-free diets. A standard single-serve bottle is 500 ml. In Singapore, it is commonly served as an accompaniment to meals at restaurants and cafes, and is available through multiple food delivery services. The product is classified as a processed bottled water due to the addition of minerals, distinguishing it from plain tap water or natural mineral water.
common_user_queries: How many calories does Dasani water have?
Is Dasani water good for diabetics?
Can I drink Dasani water if I have high blood pressure?
What minerals are in Dasani water?
What is the sodium content of Dasani water?
Is Dasani water the same as mineral water?
Where can I buy Dasani water in Singapore?

confidence_reason: Dasani is a well-documented commercial product with a publicly available nutrition label. Macronutrient values (all zero except trace sodium and potassium from added minerals) are confirmed by the USDA FoodData Central entry for drinking water and cross-referenced with the Coca-Cola/Dasani product label. No estimation of macronutrients was required.
notes: All core macros (energy_kcal, protein_g, fat_g, carbohydrate_g) are confirmed zero: energy_kcal is naturally zero as this is pure water with no caloric content; protein_g contains no protein by product formulation and is naturally zero; fat_g contains no fat by product formulation and is naturally zero; carbohydrate_g contains no carbohydrates by product formulation and is naturally zero. Confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central and Dasani product label. Sugar_g, saturated_fat_g, monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, dietary_fibre_g, and cholesterol_mg are all confirmed zero by product formulation. Sodium_mg (~4.2 mg per 100 g) and potassium_mg (~1.6 mg per 100 g) are derived from the Dasani added mineral blend (magnesium sulfate, potassium chloride, salt) as reported on the product label. calcium_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the primary source. iron_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the primary source. vitamin_c_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the primary source. vitamin_a_mcg_rae is -1 as it is not reported in the primary source. vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the primary source. vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the primary source. vitamin_b3_niacin_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the primary source. vitamin_b6_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the primary source. vitamin_b12_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the primary source. folate_mcg_dfe is -1 as it is not reported in the primary source. vitamin_d_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the primary source. vitamin_e_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the primary source. phosphorus_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the primary source. magnesium_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the primary source (magnesium sulfate is present as an additive but quantitative mg per 100 g is not disclosed on the label). zinc_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the primary source. selenium_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the primary source. glycemic_index set to 1 (effectively zero) as this beverage contains no glycemic carbohydrates; gi_category low per convention. field survey data listing at Khao - Funan at SGD 3.00 per bottle preserved from input record. Multilingual names provided for Singaporean context (English, Chinese Simplified, Chinese Traditional, Malay, Tamil, Hindi) as the food is sold in Singapore.

Added zero-macro justification phrases in notes for all four core macros per zero-macro justification rule.
Documented all -1 sentinel values in notes with explicit reason and source per sentinel values documentation rule.
Set ontology match_type fields to 'closest_parent' for all three ontology systems per ontology match_type rule.
